prompt: A projectile is launched from ground level at 50 m/s at 40 degrees above horizontal. It experiences quadratic air drag with deceleration a_drag = c * |v| * v (componentwise), where c = 0.01 per metre and g = 9.81 m/s^2. How far (horizontal range, in metres) does it travel before returning to the ground?
rationale: Quadratic drag has no closed-form range. Reaching for algebra ('code') yields the vacuum range (~250.97 m), badly outside tolerance; only time-stepped simulation gets it right. Cleanly discriminates simulation from code.
